mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of managing model deployments.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the required CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs vLLM in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage LLM serving easily.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM resource to demonstrate how to deploy a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates an example vLLM resource using the GPT-2 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify vLLM Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> You should see the 'example-vllm' resource listed with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes vLLM and its associated resources from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls vLLM from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This process involves draining the existing pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain example-vllm --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl uncordon example-vllm

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> This command drains the existing vLLM pod.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> This command uncordons the pod to allow scheduling.
> Verify that the upgraded vLLM is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm.io/v1' not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'example-vllm' is not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be in a CrashLoopBackOff state due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or be accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME            READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE' followed by your vLLM resource with a status of 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
